businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and Physical AI Platform spanning autonomous driving, robotics, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY) with net income down 46.5%. Closed 3/6 at $396.73 (-2.17%) as jobs shock and oil $90+ amplified macro selling pressure. France and Norway saw February market share recovery while Germany's Gigafactory faces IG Metall union elections. NHTSA FSD data submission deadline is 3/9 (D-1). Morgan Stanley calls it a 'coiled spring.' P/E 392.38x, Forward P/E 191.85x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack utility storage
Services/Other: ~13% of revenue ($12.5B). Supercharger network, insurance, maintenance
FSD subscription: $99/month autonomous driving software (subscription-only since Feb 2026)
keyProducts: Cybertruck (base AWD trim, deliveries pushed to 2027 due to demand surge)
Optimus (humanoid robot, Fremont factory conversion underway)
Megapack (utility energy storage, Megapack 3 and Mega Block launching 2026)

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extremely high vs. earnings. Reflects AI/autonomy premium

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Still very elevated even on forward estimates

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue declined. EV competition intensifying

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Sharply compressed from price cuts and cost increases

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Return on equity plunged, reflecting profitability decline

keyMetrics.name: Debt/Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Very healthy balance sheet. Net cash $29.3B

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD Data Deadline D-1 — March 9 Regulatory Inflection
growthDrivers.description: Final FSD crash data submission due Monday 3/9 after two extensions. Pass accelerates autonomy approval; fail risks 30% downside (Nasdaq analysis). Everything hinges on Monday.

growthDrivers.title: France & Norway February Market Share Rebound — Europe Stabilizing
growthDrivers.description: Reuters: Tesla regained market share in France and Norway in February. Stabilization signal after two consecutive years of European sales decline. Contrasts with UK's -37%.

growthDrivers.title: German Gigafactory Union Election — IG Metall vs Tesla
growthDrivers.description: Reuters: Germany's largest industrial union IG Metall seeks to expand influence at Berlin Gigafactory. New works council vote amid labor disputes and legal battles.

growthDrivers.title: BofA Maintains Buy $460, Reinforces Autonomy Leadership
growthDrivers.description: Bank of America resumed coverage on 3/4 with Buy rating and $460 target (+16% upside). Called Tesla 'the current leader in consumer autonomy.' BNP Paribas cut to $280.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader, though surpassed globally by BYD. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy, and 8.2B FSD miles is the core moat.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelming training data advantage vs competitors.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 2025 global EV sales leader. Rapid European growth, US limited by tariffs

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 SUV ($45,000) launching 2026 to compete directly with Model Y

competitors.name: Xiaomi
competitors.detail: YU7 SUV outselling Model Y 2:1 in China

recentNews.headline: NHTSA FSD Data Deadline D-1 — Final Submission Due March 9
recentNews.significance: After two extensions, Monday is the final deadline. Could determine Tesla's entire 2026 trajectory.

recentNews.headline: Tesla Stock Tests $400 Support — Tariffs, Jobs Shock Compound Selling
recentNews.significance: Global macro selloff combined with Tesla-specific headwinds drove shares to multi-month lows.

recentNews.headline: BofA Resumes Tesla Coverage at Buy with $460 Target
recentNews.significance: Upgraded from Hold to Buy. Cites consumer autonomy leadership with +16% upside.

recentNews.headline: IG Metall Targets Breakthrough at Tesla's Berlin Gigafactory
recentNews.significance: New works council election amid labor disputes. Potential cost and operational disruption risks.

recentNews.headline: Tesla Regains Market Share in France and Norway in February
recentNews.significance: Stabilization after two years of European sales decline. UK -37% remains a concern.

analystOpinions.notableComment: BofA: 'Current leader in consumer autonomy, Buy $460.' Motley Fool: 'Cheapest Forward P/E in a year.' BNP Paribas: Cut to $280 on demand concerns.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Deadline D-1 (March 9)
risks.description: Final FSD crash data submission deadline Monday. Adverse ruling could devastate the autonomy narrative with 30% downside risk. No more extensions likely.

risks.title: Global EV Competition Intensifying — BYD, Xiaomi, Rivian
risks.description: BYD is global #1, Xiaomi YU7 outsells Model Y 2:1 in China, Rivian R2 launching 2026. Market share defense increasingly difficult.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation Premium
risks.description: P/E 392x, Forward P/E 192x. Any growth deceleration risks severe multiple compression.

risks.title: 25% Tariff + Oil $90+ Macro Headwinds
risks.description: H200 chip tariffs and Iran conflict-driven oil surge weigh on consumer sentiment and EV demand.

risks.title: German Labor Conflicts and EU Regulatory Credit Erosion
risks.description: IG Metall expanding influence at Berlin Gigafactory. Toyota/Stellantis EU CO2 pool exit reduces credit revenue.

buyReasons.title: Autonomous Driving Data Leadership — 8.2B FSD Miles
buyReasons.rationale: Competitive advantage confirmed by BofA. Regulatory approval could unlock explosive robotaxi growth.

buyReasons.title: Energy Business +27% YoY High Growth
buyReasons.rationale: Megapack demand surging, Megapack 3 launching 2026. Growth engine offsetting EV slowdown.

buyReasons.title: Net Cash $29.3B — Financial Safety Net
buyReasons.rationale: Debt/equity 0.18x, $44B cash. Ample investment capacity despite headwinds.

buyReasons.title: European Market Stabilization Signal
buyReasons.rationale: France and Norway February rebound. Possible bottom forming after two years of decline.

overallOpinion: Tesla sits at $396.73, barely clinging to 200-day SMA support at $392. The weekend passes and Monday brings the NHTSA FSD deadline — a binary event for the stock.
BofA Buy $460 vs BNP $280 — Wall Street has rarely been this divided on a single stock. This reflects pure binary risk: autonomy success or failure.
France and Norway rebounds signal a European floor, but UK -37% and China's Xiaomi threat remain serious concerns.
P/E 392x is unjustifiable on current earnings alone. FSD approval + robotaxi monetization is the only multiple defense.
Near-term, everything rides on the March 9 NHTSA outcome. Pass = rally toward $450+. Fail = 200-day SMA break and further downside.

sources.name: Yahoo Finance
sources.description: Real-time TSLA price, market cap, volume

threads.text: $TSLA $396.73 (-2.17%). Sitting 1% above 200-day SMA $392. Monday 3/9 NHTSA FSD deadline — final, no more extensions. BofA says $460, BNP says $280 — Wall Street hasn't been this split in years. France/Norway rebounding, UK still -37%. P/E 392x only makes sense if FSD passes. Pass = $450+. Fail = $350. What would you do over the weekend? investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-08
